name: Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Lie on a flat bench, grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width, unrack and lower the bar to your mid-chest, then press it back up to full lockout.
tips: Retract shoulder blades and arch upper back
Keep feet flat on the floor
Lower the bar with control to mid-chest

name: Incline Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Set the bench to a 30-45 degree incline, unrack the barbell with a shoulder-width grip and lower it to your upper chest, then press back up.
tips: Keep your back pressed into the bench
Use a slightly narrower grip than flat bench
Lower the bar to upper chest near the collarbone

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
instructions: Lie on a flat bench holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level, press them up until your arms are fully extended, then lower back down with control.
tips: Keep a neutral wrist position
Allow a deeper stretch at the bottom than barbell bench
Press the dumbbells slightly inward at the top

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
instructions: Set the bench to 30-45 degrees, press dumbbells from shoulder level upward until arms are extended, focusing on squeezing the upper chest.
tips: Don't set the incline too steep or shoulders will take over
Keep elbows at roughly 45 degrees
Control the descent for a full stretch

name: Decline Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Secure your legs on a decline bench, unrack the barbell and lower it to your lower chest, then press it back up to lockout.
tips: Use a spotter for safety
Keep the bar path slightly curved toward your lower chest
Don't go too heavy without a spotter

name: Dumbbell Fly
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with dumbbells extended above your chest, lower them out to the sides in a wide arc with a slight bend in your elbows, then bring them back together.
tips: Maintain a slight bend in your elbows throughout
Focus on stretching the chest at the bottom
Don't go too heavy as this stresses the shoulder joint

name: Cable Fly
instructions: Stand between two cable pulleys set at chest height, grab the handles and step forward, then bring your hands together in front of your chest with a slight elbow bend.
tips: Lean slightly forward to engage the chest more
Squeeze at the peak contraction
Keep constant tension throughout the movement

name: Machine Chest Press
instructions: Sit in the chest press machine with handles at chest height, press the handles forward until your arms are extended, then return slowly.
tips: Adjust the seat so handles align with mid-chest
Don't lock out elbows completely
Focus on squeezing the chest at full extension

name: Pec Deck Machine
instructions: Sit in the pec deck with your forearms against the pads, bring the pads together in front of your chest by squeezing your pecs, then return slowly.
tips: Keep your back flat against the pad
Don't let the weight stack slam at the bottom
Focus on the squeeze at peak contraction

name: Push-ups
instructions: Start in a plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor, then push back up.
tips: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels
Engage your core throughout
Don't flare your elbows too wide

name: Dips (Chest)
instructions: Grip parallel bars and lean your torso forward about 30 degrees, lower your body until you feel a stretch in the chest, then push back up.
tips: Lean forward to target the chest more
Go to at least 90 degrees of elbow flexion
Add weight with a belt once bodyweight becomes easy

name: Cable Crossover
instructions: Set cables to the highest position, grab handles and step forward, then bring your hands down and together in a sweeping arc, crossing slightly at the bottom.
tips: Keep a slight bend in your elbows
Step forward enough to feel a stretch at the start
Cross your hands slightly at the bottom for peak contraction

name: Barbell Row
instructions: Hinge at the hips with a slight knee bend, grip the barbell overhand and pull it toward your lower chest/upper abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
tips: Keep your back flat and core braced
Pull with your elbows, not your hands
Don't use excessive body English

name: Dumbbell Row
instructions: Place one knee and hand on a bench for support, row a dumbbell from a dead hang up to your hip with the other arm, squeezing the lat at the top.
tips: Keep your torso parallel to the floor
Drive your elbow past your torso at the top
Avoid rotating your torso as you row

name: Pull-ups
instructions: Hang from a bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width, pull yourself up until your chin clears the bar, then lower with control.
tips: Initiate the pull by depressing your shoulder blades
Avoid kipping or swinging
Go all the way down to a dead hang each rep

name: Chin-ups
instructions: Hang from a bar with an underhand (supinated) grip at shoulder width, pull yourself up until your chin is over the bar, then lower slowly.
tips: Supinated grip places more emphasis on biceps
Keep your core engaged to prevent swinging
Full range of motion from dead hang to chin over bar

name: Lat Pulldown
instructions: Sit at a lat pulldown machine, grip the bar wider than shoulder-width, pull it down to your upper chest while squeezing your lats, then return with control.
tips: Lean back slightly and drive elbows down and back
Don't pull behind the neck
Control the eccentric phase

name: Seated Cable Row
instructions: Sit at a cable row station with feet on the platform, pull the handle to your abdomen by retracting your shoulder blades, then extend your arms back out slowly.
tips: Keep your chest up and avoid rounding your back
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of each rep
Don't lean too far back

name: T-Bar Row
instructions: Straddle the T-bar or landmine attachment, hinge at the hips and grip the handles, row the weight up toward your chest while keeping your back flat.
tips: Keep your knees slightly bent
Squeeze your back at the top of the movement
Avoid rounding your lower back

name: Face Pull
instructions: Set a cable to upper chest height with a rope attachment, pull the rope toward your face by driving your elbows back and externally rotating your shoulders.
tips: Pull the rope apart as it comes toward your face
Keep your elbows high
Squeeze your rear delts and upper back at the end

name: Meadows Row
instructions: Stand perpendicular to a landmine barbell, stagger your stance with the near leg back, grip the end of the barbell overhand and row it up to your hip.
tips: Use a staggered stance with the inside leg forward
Drive your elbow up and back
Get a full stretch at the bottom

name: Straight-Arm Pulldown
instructions: Stand facing a high cable with a straight bar or rope, keeping arms nearly straight, pull the bar down in an arc to your thighs by engaging your lats.
tips: Keep a slight bend in your elbows
Squeeze your lats hard at the bottom
Lean slightly forward at the hips for better range of motion

name: Machine Row
instructions: Sit at a row machine with your chest against the pad, grip the handles and pull them toward your torso by squeezing your shoulder blades together.
tips: Adjust the chest pad so your arms can fully extend
Don't lean back excessively
Focus on the mind-muscle connection with your back

name: Pull-Up Bar Inverted Row
instructions: Set a barbell in a rack at about waist height, hang underneath it with an overhand grip and heels on the floor, then pull your chest up to the bar.
tips: Keep your body in a straight line like a plank
Squeeze your shoulder blades at the top
Lower the bar height to increase difficulty

name: Deadlift
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, hinge at the hips to grip the barbell, brace your core, then drive through your feet to stand up with the barbell, keeping it close to your body.
tips: Keep the bar close to your body throughout
Push the floor away rather than pulling the bar up
Do not round your lower back
Lock out by squeezing glutes at the top

name: Rack Pull
instructions: Set a barbell on safety pins at just below knee height, hinge at the hips and grip the bar, then stand up by driving your hips forward and squeezing your back.
tips: Set the pins at or just below the knee
Focus on the lockout and squeeze your traps
Use straps if grip is the limiting factor

name: Overhead Press (Barbell)
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, unrack the barbell at shoulder height, then press it overhead to full lockout while keeping your core tight.
tips: Brace your core and squeeze your glutes
Move your head out of the bar path, not the bar around your head
Lock out fully at the top

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
instructions: Sit on an upright bench with dumbbells at shoulder height, palms facing forward, press them overhead until arms are extended, then lower back down.
tips: Keep your back against the bench for support
Don't lock out aggressively at the top
Control the descent to shoulder level

name: Arnold Press
instructions: Start with dumbbells in front of your shoulders with palms facing you, rotate your palms outward as you press the dumbbells overhead, then reverse the motion on the way down.
tips: Perform the rotation smoothly throughout the press
Don't rush the movement
Use lighter weight than a standard shoulder press

name: Lateral Raise (Dumbbell)
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides, raise them out to the sides until your arms are parallel with the floor, then lower slowly.
tips: Lead with your elbows, not your hands
Slight bend in the elbows throughout
Don't swing or use momentum

name: Cable Lateral Raise
instructions: Stand sideways to a low cable, grab the handle with your far hand, and raise your arm out to the side until parallel with the floor, then lower with control.
tips: Cables provide constant tension unlike dumbbells
Keep a slight lean away from the cable for a better range
Control the negative for more time under tension

name: Front Raise
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells in front of your thighs, raise one or both arms straight in front of you to shoulder height, then lower slowly.
tips: Keep a slight bend in your elbows
Don't swing the weight up
Alternate arms or raise both simultaneously

name: Rear Delt Fly (Dumbbell)
instructions: Bend forward at the hips holding dumbbells, raise them out to the sides by squeezing your rear delts, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
tips: Keep your torso nearly parallel to the floor
Don't use momentum to swing the weights
Squeeze at the top for a one-second hold

name: Cable Rear Delt Fly
instructions: Stand between two cables set at shoulder height, cross-grip the handles and pull them apart by driving your elbows back and squeezing your rear delts.
tips: Keep your arms at shoulder height
Focus on squeezing the rear delts
Use a light weight for proper form

name: Machine Shoulder Press
instructions: Sit in a shoulder press machine with handles at shoulder height, press the handles overhead to full extension, then lower back to the start.
tips: Adjust the seat so handles start at shoulder level
Keep your back pressed into the pad
Don't fully lock out to keep tension on the delts

name: Upright Row
instructions: Hold a barbell with a shoulder-width grip, pull it straight up along your body to about chin height, leading with your elbows, then lower slowly.
tips: Use a wider grip to reduce shoulder impingement risk
Don't pull higher than your shoulders
Lead with your elbows, not your hands

name: Face Pull (Rear Delts)
instructions: Set a cable at face height with a rope, pull toward your face while externally rotating your hands outward at the end, focusing on rear delt engagement.
tips: Externally rotate at the end to activate rear delts fully
Use a lighter weight for higher reps
Keep your elbows high throughout the pull

name: Barbell Shrug
instructions: Hold a barbell at arm's length with an overhand grip, shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears, squeeze at the top, then lower slowly.
tips: Shrug straight up, don't roll your shoulders
Hold the top position for a second
Use straps if grip limits the weight you can use

name: Barbell Curl
instructions: Stand with a shoulder-width underhand grip on a barbell, curl the bar up to shoulder height by bending your elbows, then lower with control.
tips: Keep your elbows pinned at your sides
Don't swing your body to generate momentum
Squeeze at the top and control the negative

name: Dumbbell Curl
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides with palms facing forward, curl the weights up to shoulder level by bending your elbows, then lower slowly.
tips: Supinate your wrist as you curl for maximum bicep activation
Alternate arms or curl both simultaneously
Avoid swinging the weights up

name: Hammer Curl
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides with palms facing each other (neutral grip), curl the weights up keeping the neutral grip throughout, then lower slowly.
tips: Neutral grip targets the brachialis and brachioradialis
Keep your elbows stationary
Can be performed alternating or simultaneously

name: Preacher Curl
instructions: Sit at a preacher bench with your upper arms resting on the pad, curl the bar up by bending your elbows, then lower all the way back down with control.
tips: Don't let your arms go completely straight at the bottom to avoid bicep strain
Keep your upper arms flat on the pad throughout
Use an EZ bar to reduce wrist strain

name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
instructions: Sit on a bench set to a 45-degree incline with dumbbells hanging at arm's length, curl the weights up while keeping your upper arms stationary.
tips: The incline puts a greater stretch on the long head of the bicep
Keep your upper arms perpendicular to the floor
Use lighter weight than standing curls

name: Cable Curl
instructions: Stand facing a low cable with a straight or EZ bar attachment, curl the bar up to shoulder height while keeping your elbows at your sides, then lower slowly.
tips: Cables maintain constant tension throughout the rep
Keep your elbows pinned to your sides
Squeeze at the top contraction

name: EZ Bar Curl
instructions: Stand gripping an EZ curl bar on the angled handles, curl the bar up to shoulder level while keeping elbows stationary, then lower with control.
tips: The angled grip reduces stress on the wrists
Use the inner or outer grip to vary emphasis
Keep strict form and avoid swinging

name: Concentration Curl
instructions: Sit on a bench, rest your elbow against your inner thigh, curl a dumbbell up toward your shoulder, squeezing the bicep at the top, then lower slowly.
tips: Brace your elbow firmly against your thigh
Focus on the peak contraction at the top
This isolates the bicep extremely well with no momentum

name: Tricep Pushdown
instructions: Stand at a cable machine with a straight bar or rope at chest height, push the bar down by extending your elbows until arms are straight, then return slowly.
tips: Keep your elbows pinned at your sides
Don't lean forward excessively
Squeeze the triceps at full extension

name: Overhead Tricep Extension (Cable)
instructions: Face away from a low cable with a rope attachment held overhead, extend your forearms forward and up by straightening your elbows, then lower back behind your head.
tips: Keep your upper arms close to your head
Get a full stretch on the tricep at the bottom
Keep your elbows pointing forward

name: Skull Crushers
instructions: Lie on a flat bench holding a barbell or EZ bar with arms extended over your chest, lower the bar toward your forehead by bending only at the elbows, then extend back up.
tips: Lower the bar to your forehead or just behind your head for a greater stretch
Keep your upper arms perpendicular to the floor
Use an EZ bar to reduce wrist strain

name: Close-Grip Bench Press
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with hands shoulder-width apart or slightly narrower, lower the bar to your lower chest, then press back up focusing on tricep engagement.
tips: Keep your grip shoulder-width, not too narrow
Tuck your elbows close to your body
This is also a great chest builder

name: Tricep Dips
instructions: Grip parallel bars and keep your torso upright, lower your body by bending your elbows until they reach 90 degrees, then push back up to lockout.
tips: Stay upright to emphasize the triceps over the chest
Don't go too deep to avoid shoulder strain
Add weight with a belt for progressive overload

name: Dumbbell Kickback
instructions: Hinge forward and hold a dumbbell with your upper arm parallel to your torso, extend your forearm back until your arm is straight, then return slowly.
tips: Keep your upper arm stationary and parallel to the floor
Squeeze the tricep at full extension
Use a light weight and focus on form

name: Cable Overhead Extension
instructions: Stand facing away from a cable with a rope attachment overhead, extend your arms forward by straightening at the elbows, then let the rope return behind your head.
tips: Step forward to create tension at the start position
Keep elbows close to your head
Focus on the long head of the tricep with the overhead position

name: Diamond Push-ups
instructions: Get into a push-up position with your hands close together forming a diamond shape with your thumbs and index fingers, lower your chest to your hands, then push back up.
tips: Keep your elbows close to your body
Maintain a straight body line
Wider hand placement makes it easier if you need to regress

name: Barbell Back Squat
instructions: Position a barbell on your upper back, unrack it and step back, then squat down by bending your knees and hips until your thighs are at least parallel, then stand back up.
tips: Keep your chest up and core braced
Push your knees out over your toes
Drive through your whole foot, not just your heels
Go to at least parallel depth

name: Front Squat
instructions: Rest a barbell on the front of your shoulders in a clean grip or cross-arm grip, squat down keeping your torso very upright, then stand back up.
tips: Keep your elbows high to prevent the bar from rolling
This demands more ankle and thoracic mobility than back squats
Go as deep as your mobility allows

name: Leg Press
instructions: Sit in the leg press machine with your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform, lower the sled by bending your knees, then press it back up without locking your knees.
tips: Don't let your lower back round off the pad at the bottom
Place feet higher for more glute and hamstring emphasis
Never fully lock out your knees

name: Hack Squat
instructions: Position yourself in the hack squat machine with your back against the pad and feet shoulder-width apart, squat down until thighs are parallel, then press back up.
tips: Keep your back flat against the pad throughout
Lower foot placement emphasizes quads more
Use full range of motion for best results

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions: Stand a couple feet in front of a bench, place the top of your rear foot on the bench behind you, hold dumbbells and squat down on your front leg until your thigh is parallel.
tips: Keep your torso upright
Most of your weight should be on the front foot
Don't let your front knee cave inward

name: Walking Lunges
instructions: Hold dumbbells at your sides or use bodyweight, step forward into a lunge until both knees are at about 90 degrees, push off the front foot and step the back leg forward into the next lunge.
tips: Take long enough steps that your front knee stays behind your toe line
Keep your torso upright
Push off through the heel of the front foot

name: Leg Extension
instructions: Sit in the leg extension machine with the pad on your shins just above your ankles, extend your legs until they are straight, squeeze your quads, then lower slowly.
tips: Adjust the pad so it sits just above your ankles
Pause at the top for a peak contraction
Control the negative to maximize time under tension

name: Goblet Squat
instructions: Hold a dumbbell or kettlebell at chest height with both hands, squat down between your legs until your elbows touch your knees, then stand back up.
tips: Keep the weight close to your chest
Push your knees out as you descend
Great for learning proper squat mechanics

name: Step-ups
instructions: Hold dumbbells at your sides, step up onto a bench or box with one foot, drive through that heel to stand fully on top, then step back down with control.
tips: Use a box height where your thigh is parallel at the start
Don't push off with the back foot
Keep your torso upright throughout

name: Sissy Squat
instructions: Stand on your toes, lean your torso back while bending at the knees, lowering your body until your knees are fully bent and your torso is angled back, then push back up.
tips: Hold onto something for balance as you learn
Keep your hips extended throughout
This heavily isolates the quads with a deep stretch

name: Romanian Deadlift (Barbell)
instructions: Hold a barbell at hip height, push your hips back while keeping a slight knee bend, lower the bar along your legs until you feel a deep hamstring stretch, then drive your hips forward to stand.
tips: Keep the bar close to your legs the entire time
Push your hips back, don't bend your back
Stop when you feel a stretch, don't force extra range of motion

name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Hold dumbbells in front of your thighs, hinge at the hips and push them back while lowering the dumbbells along your legs, then drive your hips forward to return.
tips: Keep a slight bend in your knees
Dumbbells allow a slightly different path than a barbell
Focus on feeling the hamstring stretch

name: Lying Leg Curl
instructions: Lie face-down on a leg curl machine with the pad behind your ankles, curl your heels toward your glutes by bending your knees, then lower with control.
tips: Don't lift your hips off the pad
Squeeze your hamstrings at the top
Control the eccentric phase for maximum muscle activation

name: Seated Leg Curl
instructions: Sit in the leg curl machine with the pad on the back of your lower legs, curl your legs under the seat by bending your knees, then extend back slowly.
tips: Adjust the back pad so you feel a stretch at the start
Don't let the weight slam on the return
Squeeze your hamstrings at peak contraction

name: Nordic Curl
instructions: Kneel on a pad with your ankles anchored, slowly lower your torso forward by extending at the knees while keeping your hips extended, catch yourself and curl back up.
tips: Start with negatives only if you cannot do the full movement
Keep your hips extended, don't bend at the waist
This is one of the best exercises for hamstring injury prevention

name: Good Morning
instructions: Place a barbell on your upper back like a squat, then hinge forward at the hips with a slight knee bend until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor, then stand back up.
tips: Use a lighter weight than you would for squats
Keep your back flat throughout
Push your hips back, don't just bend forward

name: Stiff-Leg Deadlift
instructions: Stand holding a barbell with legs nearly straight, hinge forward at the hips lowering the bar toward your feet while keeping legs stiff, then return by driving your hips forward.
tips: Keep your legs straighter than an RDL for more hamstring emphasis
Don't round your back
Only go as low as your hamstring flexibility allows

name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell in the opposite hand, hinge forward at the hip while extending the free leg behind you, then return to standing.
tips: Keep your hips square to the floor
The back leg and torso should move as one unit
Use a wall or rack for balance if needed

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench, roll a barbell over your hips, then drive your hips up until your thighs are parallel to the floor, squeezing your glutes at the top.
tips: Use a barbell pad for comfort
Keep your chin tucked to maintain a neutral spine
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top and hold for a second

name: Glute Bridge March
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, drive your hips up by squeezing your glutes until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
tips: Squeeze your glutes at the top and hold briefly
Push through your heels
Add a dumbbell on your hips for extra resistance

name: Cable Kickback
instructions: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable, face the machine, and kick your leg straight back behind you by squeezing your glute, then return slowly.
tips: Keep your core braced and avoid arching your lower back
Squeeze the glute at full extension
Control the movement in both directions

name: Sumo Deadlift
instructions: Stand with a wide stance and toes pointed out, grip the barbell between your legs with arms inside your knees, then drive through your feet to stand up with the bar.
tips: Push your knees out over your toes
Keep your chest up and back flat
The wider stance emphasizes glutes and adductors more than conventional

name: Cable Pull-Through
instructions: Stand facing away from a low cable with a rope between your legs, hinge at the hips to let the cable pull your hands back, then drive your hips forward to stand up straight.
tips: This is a great hip hinge teaching tool
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top
Don't pull with your arms, drive with your hips

name: Banded Clamshell
instructions: Lie on your side with a resistance band around both knees, keep your feet together and open your top knee like a clamshell, squeezing your glute, then close slowly.
tips: Keep your feet stacked together throughout
Don't rotate your hips backward as you open
Great for warm-ups and glute activation

name: Reverse Lunge
instructions: Stand tall holding dumbbells at your sides, step one foot backward and lower your back knee toward the ground, then push through your front foot to return to standing.
tips: Keep your torso upright throughout
Step far enough back that both knees reach 90 degrees
Push off the front heel to engage the glutes

name: Standing Calf Raise
instructions: Stand on a calf raise machine with the balls of your feet on the edge of the platform, raise your heels as high as possible, then lower slowly below the platform for a full stretch.
tips: Get a full stretch at the bottom and a full squeeze at the top
Pause for a second at the top of each rep
Keep your legs straight to target the gastrocnemius

name: Seated Calf Raise
instructions: Sit in a calf raise machine with the pads on your lower thighs, place the balls of your feet on the platform, and raise your heels as high as possible, then lower slowly.
tips: The seated position targets the soleus muscle more
Use a slow tempo for maximum time under tension
Get a full stretch at the bottom of each rep

name: Leg Press Calf Raise
instructions: Sit in a leg press machine with just the balls of your feet on the bottom edge of the platform, extend your ankles to push the sled up, then lower slowly.
tips: Use a lighter weight than you would for leg press
Keep the safety catches engaged
Full range of motion is key for calf development

name: Donkey Calf Raise
instructions: Bend at the hips with your torso parallel to the floor on a donkey calf raise machine, place the balls of your feet on the edge of the platform, raise your heels as high as possible.
tips: The hip-flexed position provides a greater calf stretch
Can also be done with a partner on your back if no machine
Focus on the stretch and squeeze at each end

name: Plank
instructions: Support your body on your forearms and toes in a straight line from head to heels, brace your core and hold the position for the prescribed duration.
tips: Don't let your hips sag or pike up
Squeeze your glutes and brace your abs
Breathe normally while holding the position

name: Cable Crunch
instructions: Kneel in front of a cable machine with a rope attachment overhead, hold the rope beside your head and crunch downward by flexing your spine, then return with control.
tips: Focus on curling your spine, not just hinging at the hips
Keep the rope at the same position relative to your head
Exhale as you crunch down

name: Hanging Leg Raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with straight arms, raise your legs up in front of you by flexing at the hips and curling your pelvis, then lower with control.
tips: Avoid swinging by keeping the movement controlled
Curl your pelvis at the top for full ab engagement
Bend your knees to make it easier

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
instructions: Kneel on the floor gripping an ab wheel, roll it forward extending your body as far as you can while keeping your core tight, then pull back to the starting position.
tips: Start with partial range of motion and build up
Keep your core braced and don't let your hips sag
Squeeze your abs to pull yourself back

name: Woodchop (Cable)
instructions: Set a cable to a high position, grip the handle with both hands and pull it diagonally across your body from high to low by rotating your torso, then return with control.
tips: Rotate through your torso, not just your arms
Keep your arms relatively straight
Can be done high-to-low or low-to-high

name: Russian Twist
instructions: Sit on the floor with knees bent and feet slightly elevated, lean back slightly and rotate your torso from side to side, touching the floor beside each hip.
tips: Keep your chest up as you rotate
Add weight by holding a dumbbell or medicine ball
Lift your feet to increase difficulty

name: Bicycle Crunch
instructions: Lie on your back with hands behind your head, bring one knee toward your chest while rotating your opposite elbow toward it, alternating sides in a pedaling motion.
tips: Don't pull on your neck with your hands
Focus on the rotation, not just the crunch
Fully extend each leg as you alternate

name: Dead Bug
instructions: Lie on your back with arms extended toward the ceiling and knees bent at 90 degrees, slowly lower opposite arm and leg toward the floor while keeping your lower back pressed down.
tips: Keep your lower back firmly pressed into the floor
Move slowly and with control
Exhale as you extend your limbs

name: Pallof Press
instructions: Stand sideways to a cable set at chest height, hold the handle at your chest, then press it straight out in front of you, resisting the rotation, and return to your chest.
tips: Don't let the cable rotate your torso
Brace your core hard as you extend your arms
This is an anti-rotation exercise, so resist the pull

name: Decline Sit-up
instructions: Hook your feet under the pads of a decline bench, lie back fully, then sit up by flexing your spine until your torso is upright, and lower back down with control.
tips: Cross your arms over your chest or hold a plate for resistance
Focus on curling your spine up rather than hinging at the hips
Control the descent to avoid using momentum

name: Dumbbell Shrug
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides, shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears, squeeze at the top, then lower slowly.
tips: Don't roll your shoulders, shrug straight up
Hold the top position for a one-second squeeze
Use straps if your grip gives out before your traps

name: Farmer's Walk
instructions: Pick up heavy dumbbells or kettlebells in each hand, stand tall with your shoulders back, and walk forward with controlled, steady steps for the prescribed distance or time.
tips: Keep your shoulders back and down
Take short, controlled steps
Brace your core as if someone is about to punch you in the stomach

name: Power Clean
instructions: Start with a barbell on the floor in a deadlift position, explosively pull the bar up by extending your hips and knees, then catch the bar on your front shoulders in a partial front squat.
tips: This is a technical lift that benefits from coaching
The power comes from hip extension, not arm pulling
Keep the bar close to your body throughout
Practice with light weight until your form is solid

name: Wrist Curl
instructions: Sit with your forearms resting on your thighs and wrists hanging over your knees, hold a barbell or dumbbells palms up, curl your wrists upward, then lower slowly.
tips: Use a light weight and high reps for forearms
Let the bar roll to your fingertips for extra range of motion
Keep your forearms flat on your thighs

name: Reverse Wrist Curl
instructions: Sit with your forearms on your thighs and wrists over your knees palms facing down, extend your wrists upward lifting the bar, then lower slowly.
tips: Use an even lighter weight than regular wrist curls
This targets the wrist extensors on top of the forearm
Perform slow, controlled reps

name: Trap Bar Deadlift
instructions: Stand inside a trap bar (hex bar), grip the handles at your sides, brace your core and stand up by driving through your feet, then lower back down with control.
tips: The neutral grip makes this easier on the lower back than conventional deadlifts
Push the floor away with your feet
Keep your chest up and core braced

name: Kettlebell Swing
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a kettlebell with both hands, hinge at the hips to swing the bell between your legs, then explosively drive your hips forward to swing it to chest height.
tips: The power comes from your hips, not your arms
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top of each swing
Keep your arms relaxed and let the hips do the work
Brace your core at the top of each rep

name: Smith Machine Squat
instructions: Position the bar on your upper traps, set your feet slightly forward of the bar, unrack and squat down until thighs are parallel to the floor, then drive back up.
tips: Place feet slightly forward to keep knees safe
Keep chest up and core braced
Use the fixed bar path to focus on leg drive

name: Smith Machine Bench Press
instructions: Lie on a bench positioned under the Smith bar, unrack and lower the bar to your mid-chest, then press it back up to lockout.
tips: Position the bench so the bar lines up with your mid-chest
Use the fixed path as a stability aid for slightly heavier loads
Keep shoulder blades retracted

name: Smith Machine Bent-Over Row
instructions: Set the bar at knee height, hinge forward with a flat back and grip slightly wider than shoulder width, then pull the bar to your lower ribs and lower with control.
tips: Keep the back flat and core braced
Pull elbows back rather than up
Squeeze the shoulder blades at the top

name: Smith Machine Shoulder Press
instructions: Sit on an upright bench under the Smith bar, grip slightly wider than shoulder width and press the bar overhead until arms are extended, then lower with control.
tips: Avoid flaring elbows — keep them slightly forward
Press in a straight line up
Don't lock out aggressively

name: Smith Machine Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand with the Smith bar at thigh height, hinge at the hips and lower the bar along your legs to mid-shin, then drive hips forward to return to standing.
tips: Keep the bar close to the body throughout
Maintain a neutral spine
Feel the stretch in the hamstrings before reversing

name: Band Pull-Apart
instructions: Hold a resistance band with both hands at shoulder width and arms extended in front, pull the band apart by squeezing your shoulder blades together, then return slowly.
tips: Keep arms straight throughout
Squeeze rear delts and mid-back at full stretch
Move slowly and with control

name: Band Seated Row
instructions: Sit on the floor with legs extended, loop a resistance band around your feet and hold each end, pull the band toward your torso while squeezing the shoulder blades, then return slowly.
tips: Maintain an upright torso
Pull the elbows back rather than up
Pause briefly at the contraction

name: Band Chest Press
instructions: Anchor a resistance band behind you at chest height, hold each end with arms back and elbows bent, then press the bands forward until arms are fully extended.
tips: Step further away to increase resistance
Squeeze the chest at full extension
Keep core braced and torso steady

name: Band Bicep Curl
instructions: Stand on a resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart, hold each end with arms at your sides and palms forward, then curl the bands toward your shoulders.
tips: Keep the elbows pinned to your sides
Squeeze biceps at the top
Lower slowly to control the eccentric

name: Band Lateral Raise
instructions: Stand on a resistance band, hold each end at your sides with a slight bend in the elbows, then raise your arms out to the sides until parallel with the floor.
tips: Lead with the elbows, not the wrists
Keep a small bend in the elbows
Avoid shrugging — keep traps relaxed

name: Band Squat
instructions: Stand on a resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart, loop the band over your shoulders, then perform a squat keeping the band taut throughout.
tips: Use a heavy band for meaningful resistance
Drive through the heels
Keep chest up at the bottom of the squat

name: Kettlebell Goblet Squat
instructions: Hold a kettlebell at chest level with both hands, keep elbows tucked, then squat down until elbows touch the inside of your knees and drive back up.
tips: Keep the chest tall and core braced
Drive the knees out at the bottom
Push through the whole foot to stand

name: Kettlebell Clean and Press
instructions: Clean a kettlebell from the floor to the rack position at your shoulder, then press it overhead until your arm is locked out, and reverse the movement.
tips: Use the hips to drive the clean
Catch the kettlebell softly at the rack
Press in a straight line overhead

name: Single-Arm Kettlebell Row
instructions: Hinge forward with one hand on a stable surface, hold a kettlebell in the other hand with your arm extended, then pull the bell to your hip and lower with control.
tips: Keep the back flat
Pull the elbow toward the hip, not out wide
Pause at the top of each rep

name: Kettlebell Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Hold a kettlebell in front of your thighs, hinge at the hips and lower the bell along your legs until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings, then drive hips forward to stand.
tips: Keep the kettlebell close to the body
Maintain a neutral spine
Drive through the heels

name: Kettlebell Front Squat
instructions: Hold two kettlebells in the rack position at your shoulders, keep your elbows up, then squat down to depth and stand back up.
tips: Keep the elbows high to maintain torso position
Brace the core hard before each rep
Drive the knees out as you descend

name: EZ-Bar Skullcrusher
instructions: Lie on a bench holding an EZ-bar with arms extended above your chest, lower the bar to just above your forehead by bending only at the elbows, then press back up.
tips: Keep the elbows pointing forward, not flared
Move only at the elbow joint
Use a moderate weight to protect the elbows

name: EZ-Bar Overhead Tricep Extension
instructions: Hold an EZ-bar overhead with arms extended, lower the bar behind your head by bending at the elbows, then extend back up to lockout.
tips: Keep the elbows close to the head
Get a full stretch at the bottom
Avoid flaring the elbows out

name: EZ-Bar Bent-Over Row
instructions: Hold an EZ-bar with a pronated grip, hinge forward with a flat back, then pull the bar to your lower ribs and lower with control.
tips: Keep the back flat throughout
Pull elbows back, not up
Squeeze the shoulder blades at the top

name: EZ-Bar Reverse Curl
instructions: Hold an EZ-bar with a pronated (palms-down) grip, keep elbows pinned to your sides, then curl the bar up and lower with control.
tips: Keep wrists firm
Use lighter weight than a regular curl
Squeeze the forearms at the top

name: Inverted Row
instructions: Set a bar at waist height, lie under it and grip with hands shoulder-width apart, then pull your chest to the bar by squeezing your shoulder blades together.
tips: Keep the body in a straight line
Lower with control
Elevate the feet to make it harder

name: Toes-to-Bar
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with arms extended, brace the core and bring your toes up to touch the bar, then lower with control.
tips: Keep the legs as straight as you can
Avoid swinging — use a strict tempo
Build up via knee raises if needed

name: Hanging Knee Raises
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with arms extended, then bring your knees up to your chest by contracting your abs, and lower with control.
tips: Avoid swinging by bracing the core
Pause at the top for an extra contraction
Keep the descent slow

name: Archer Pull-Up
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with a wide grip, pull yourself up toward one hand while keeping the other arm straight, then lower and alternate.
tips: Keep the working side tight
Build up via assisted pull-ups
Move slowly to maintain control

name: Pistol Squat
instructions: Stand on one leg with the other extended forward, lower into a deep squat on the supporting leg, then drive back up.
tips: Use a counterbalance with arms forward
Build up via box squats first
Keep the heel grounded

name: Bodyweight Squat
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, squat down until thighs are parallel to the floor, then drive back up to standing.
tips: Keep the chest up
Drive the knees out
Push through the whole foot

name: Diamond Push-Up
instructions: Get into a push-up position with hands close together forming a diamond shape, lower your chest to your hands, then press back up.
tips: Keep the elbows close to the body
Maintain a straight body line
Lower under control

name: Decline Push-Up
instructions: Place your feet on an elevated surface and hands on the floor, lower your chest to the floor, then press back up to extension.
tips: Keep the body in a straight line
The higher the feet, the harder it is
Brace the core throughout

name: Pike Push-Up
instructions: Start in a downward dog position with hips piked and hands shoulder-width apart, lower your head toward the floor, then press back up.
tips: Keep hips piked high
Lower the head between the hands
Build toward handstand push-ups

name: Mountain Climber
instructions: Get into a high plank position, then alternate driving your knees toward your chest at a controlled, steady pace.
tips: Keep the hips down — don't pike up
Brace the core throughout
Move at a steady tempo

name: Jump Squat
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, squat down then explosively jump as high as you can, landing softly back into a squat.
tips: Land softly on the balls of your feet
Reset between reps for clean form
Use the arms to add momentum

name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
instructions: Lie on your back with one knee bent and the other leg extended, drive through the heel of the bent leg to lift your hips, squeezing the glute at the top.
tips: Keep the extended leg in line with the torso
Pause at the top to maximize contraction
Move slowly to avoid using momentum

name: Glute Bridge
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, bridge your hips up, then alternate lifting one foot at a time while keeping your hips level.
tips: Keep the hips lifted throughout
Move slowly to avoid rotating
Squeeze the planted-side glute on each march

name: Side Plank
instructions: Lie on your side, prop yourself up on one forearm with feet stacked, then lift the hips so the body forms a straight line and hold.
tips: Stack the feet for full difficulty
Keep the hips lifted
Brace the core throughout

name: High Plank
instructions: Get into the top of a push-up position with hands under shoulders, brace your core and glutes, and hold a straight line from head to heels.
tips: Push the floor away through your hands
Avoid sagging or hiking the hips
Keep breathing steady

name: Bench Tricep Dip
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with hands gripping the edge, slide your hips off and lower your body by bending the elbows, then press back up.
tips: Keep elbows pointing back, not flared
Lower until the elbows are around 90 degrees
Keep the hips close to the bench
